name: Sanisvara Nethaji Rayer A/L Rajaji
biography: Sanisvara Nethaji Rayer s/o Rajaji Rayer is a Malaysian politician and lawyer. He began his political career in 1988 after working for Karpal Singh's general election campaign and subsequently joined the Democratic Action Party (DAP). He served as the Member of the Penang State Legislative Assembly for Seri Delima from March 2008 to May 2018. In May 2018, he was elected as the Member of Parliament for Jelutong. He also serves as the Chairman of the Penang Hindu Endowments Board. Rayer was admitted to the Malaysian bar in 1995 and has his own law firm, R. Nethaji Rayer & Co. He is known for his involvement in heated arguments in Parliament and has faced controversies, including his "UMNO celaka" remarks and comments regarding TV3's broadcast license.

name: Khairil Nizam Khirudin
biography: Khairil Nizam bin Khirudin (born 16 October 1979) is a Malaysian politician and engineer. He has served as the Member of Parliament (MP) for Jerantut since November 2022. Prior to this, he was a Senator from December 2020 until November 2022. He is a member of the Malaysian Islamic Party (PAS), a component party of the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition, and has served as the Information Chief of PAS since November 2021. His past roles include Youth Chief of PN from November 2020 to January 2022 and Youth Chief of PAS from June 2019 to November 2021.

name: Larry Sng Wei Shien
biography: Datuk Larry Sng Wei Shien is a Taiwanese-born Malaysian politician who has served as the Member of Parliament (MP) for Julau since May 2018. He previously served as Member of the Sarawak State Legislative Assembly (MLA) for Pelagus from September 2001 to April 2011. He was the State Chairman of the People's Justice Party (PKR) of Sarawak before leaving to become an independent on 28 February 2021, declaring support for the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition. He is currently the President of Parti Bangsa Malaysia (PBM) since January 2022 and is the sole PBM MP, with his party aligned to the Unity Government. Born on 14 September 1979 in Taipei, his father, Sng Chee Hua, was also a politician and businessman, and his grandfather, Sng Chin Joo, was an MP and Council Negri member. He grew up in Kuala Lumpur and England, studying at the London School of Economics.

name: Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man
biography: Tuan Ibrahim bin Tuan Man, born on 27 August 1960 in Kampung Batu Balai, Jerantut, Pahang, is a Malaysian politician, lecturer, and teacher. He holds a Bachelor of Arts (Honors) Degree in Leadership and completed postgraduate studies at the National University of Malaysia in 1995. He previously worked as a teacher at SMK Clifford in Kuala Lipis and served as a senior lecturer at Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM) in Jengka, Pahang, and Manjung, Perak, for 14 years. As an Islamic religious scholar (alim), he began his political career by winning the Pahang State Legislative Assembly seat of Jengka in the 1999 election. He served as the head of PAS in Pahang since 2004 and was elected as a Vice President of PAS in 2013. In 2018, he was elected as the Member of Parliament for the Kubang Kerian constituency. He currently serves as the State Leader of the Opposition of Pahang and a Member of the Pahang State Legislative Assembly for Cheka. He has been the Deputy President of PAS since June 2015.

name: Tan Kok Wai
biography: Tan Kok Wai (born 7 October 1957) is a Malaysian politician and a member of the Democratic Action Party (DAP). He has served as the Member of Parliament (MP) for Cheras since April 1995, and previously as the MP for Sungai Besi from August 1986 to April 1995. He was appointed Special Envoy of Prime Minister Mahathir Mohamad to China and Chairman of Malaysia-China Business Council from August 2018 to March 2020. Within the DAP, he served as its 1st Advisor from March 2020 to March 2025 and its 4th National Chairman from March 2014 to March 2022. He also serves as the 1st Chairman of the Federal Territories Pakatan Harapan. He is currently the longest-serving active MP in Malaysia after Tengku Razaleigh Hamzah lost re-election in 2022.

name: Steven Sim Chee Keong
biography: Steven Sim Chee Keong, born on 13 May 1982 in Bukit Mertajam, Penang, Malaysia, is a Malaysian politician and a member of the Democratic Action Party (DAP). He graduated from the University of Malaya with a degree in Computer Science in 2004 and obtained a Masters in Sustainable Development from Sunway University in 2020. He began his political career as a member of the Seberang Perai Municipal Council in January 2011. He has been the Member of Parliament (MP) for Bukit Mertajam since May 2013. He served as the Deputy Minister of Youth and Sports from July 2018 to February 2020. He was the National Organising Secretary of DAP from March 2022 to March 2025. He served as the Deputy Minister of Finance II from December 2022 to December 2023. He currently serves as the Minister of Human Resources since December 2023. He has also served as the State Chairman of DAP of Penang since September 2024 and Deputy Secretary-General of DAP since March 2025. He is married to Chan Jo Rin and has authored 4 books.

name: Wee Jeck Seng
biography: Wee Jeck Seng, born on 20 April 1964 in Pontian, Johor, is a Malaysian politician and a member of the Malaysian Chinese Association (MCA). He holds a Bachelor of Business Management from the University of Sunderland and a Doctorate in Public Management from Universiti Utara Malaysia (2019). He began his political career as a Member of the Johor State Legislative Assembly for Pekan Nanas (2004-2008) and was first elected as the Member of Parliament for Tanjung Piai in 2008, a seat he held until 2018. During this period, he served as Deputy Minister of Youth and Sports (2008-2010). He regained the Tanjung Piai parliamentary seat in a 2019 by-election. Subsequently, he was appointed Deputy Minister of Plantation Industries and Commodities I (2020-2022). Currently, he serves as the Member of Parliament for Tanjung Piai (since November 2019) and is the Chairman-designate of Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB). He is married to Lin Ruyun.

name: Teo Nie Ching
biography: Teo Nie Ching, born on 27 January 1981, is a Malaysian politician and lawyer. She pursued her law degree from the University of the West of England in 2002 and later obtained her Certificate in Legal Practice from the University of Malaya in 2004. Her political career began with her election as the Member of Parliament for Serdang in 2008. She has since served as the MP for Kulai since 2013. In government, she served as the Deputy Minister of Education from 2018 to 2020, Deputy Minister of Communications and Digital from 2022 to 2023, and currently serves as the Deputy Minister of Communications since December 2023. A prominent member of the Democratic Action Party (DAP), she has held various party roles including International Secretary, National Publicity Secretary, Women Chief, and is designated as National Vice Chairman from March 2025. Her family has a political background, with her father, sister, and brother-in-law also involved in DAP.

name: Khlir Mohd Nor
biography: Khlir bin Mohd Nor (born 4 July 1958) is a Malaysian politician and former military officer. He has served as the Member of Parliament (MP) for Ketereh since November 2022. He is a member of the Malaysian United Indigenous Party (BERSATU), a component party of the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition. He made his electoral debut in the 2022 general election, where he won the Ketereh federal seat.

biography: Mohamad bin Alamin is a Malaysian politician and lawyer. He was born in Kg. Kelatuan, Batu Enam Kimanis, Papar, Sabah, with Kadazandusun and Brunei Malay heritage. He currently serves as the Deputy Minister of Foreign Affairs in the Unity Government administration since December 2022 and as the Member of Parliament (MP) for Kimanis since January 2020. Previously, he served as the Deputy Minister of Education II from September 2021 to November 2022 and as Chairman of the Intellectual Property Corporation of Malaysia (MyIPO) from May 2020 to March 2022. He is a member of the United Malays National Organisation (UMNO), having held positions such as Youth Chief and Vice Leader of UMNO Kimanis.

name: Fadhlina Sidek
biography: Fadhlina binti Sidek (born 16 October 1977) is a Malaysian politician and lawyer. She has served as the Minister of Education in the Unity Government administration under Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim since December 2022 and the Member of Parliament (MP) for Nibong Tebal since November 2022. She also served as a Senator from September 2021 to November 2022. She is a member of the People's Justice Party (PKR) and has served as the Women Chief of PKR since July 2022. She is the first female Minister of Education in Malaysian history. Prior to her political career, Fadhlina was an activist and lawyer specializing in Islamic family law and child welfare, and owned a Sharia law firm, Tetuan Fadhlina Siddiq & Associates. She is the daughter of the late Siddiq Fadzil, former President of the Malaysian Islamic Youth Movement (ABIM).

name: Ahmad Fadhli Shaari
biography: Ahmad Fadhli bin Shaari, born on 18 July 1980 in Pasir Mas, Kelantan, is a Malaysian politician. He has served as the Member of Parliament for Pasir Mas since May 2018. He is a member of the Malaysian Islamic Party (PAS) and a component party of the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition. He has also served as the Information Chief of PAS since November 2023. His past roles include Chairman of the Skills Development Fund Corporation (PTPK) from 2020 to 2022, Youth Chief of PN from January 2022 to January 2024, Youth Chief of PAS from November 2021 to October 2023, and Deputy Youth Chief of PAS from June 2019 to November 2021. He received his early education at Maahad Tahfiz Al-Quran Wal Qiraat Pulai Chondong, followed by studies at the Sultan Ismail Petra International Islamic College and tertiary education at al-Azhar University in Cairo, Egypt. He is known for his calls for a total ban on international concerts in Malaysia and defending the selective boycott of Western goods.

He is the second MP after Mohamed Amin Daud from Pekan since 1982 not from the Razak family.

biography: Datuk Seri Dr. Zaliha binti Mustafa (born 28 June 1964) is a Malaysian politician and physician. She has served as the Minister in the Prime Minister's Department in charge of Federal Territories since December 2023 in the Unity Government administration under Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim, making her the first woman to hold this portfolio. She previously served as Malaysia’s first female Minister of Health from December 2022 to December 2023. She has been the Member of Parliament (MP) for Sekijang since November 2022. A member of the People’s Justice Party (PKR), she has held various key positions within the party, including Chairperson of the PKR Election Committee (JPP), Member of the Central Leadership Council (2018–2020), Principal of the Keadilan Academy (2009), Deputy Chief of PKR Women’s Wing (2007–2009), and PKR Johor State Chairperson (2005–2007). She also served as Political Secretary to the Minister of Women, Family, and Community Development, Dato’ Seri Dr. Wan Azizah Wan Ismail.

biography: Ewon Benedick (born 1 August 1983) is a Malaysian politician currently serving as Minister of Entrepreneur and Cooperatives Development since December 2022. He is also the Member of Parliament (MP) for Penampang since November 2022 and Member of the Sabah State Legislative Assembly (MLA) for Kadamaian since May 2018. He previously served as State Minister of Rural Development of Sabah from May 2018 to September 2020. A member of the United Progressive Kinabalu Organisation (UPKO), a component party of the PH coalition, he has been the President of UPKO since January 2023 and the State Chairman of PH of Sabah since December 2024. He was previously Vice President of UPKO. He is the youngest Cabinet minister in the Anwar Ibrahim cabinet. Ewon won the Kadamaian state seat in 2018 and the Penampang parliamentary seat in 2022.

name: Adam Adli bin Abd Halim
position: Deputy Minister of Youth and Sports
biography: Adam Adli bin Abd Halim is a Malaysian politician and former student activist. He studied at Sultan Idris Education University, where he was suspended in 2013 for allegedly tarnishing the university's name through activism. In May 2013, he was arrested and charged under the Sedition Act for statements made at a forum; he was acquitted in February 2018. Adam Adli officially joined the People's Justice Party (PKR) on September 16, 2021. He was elected Youth Chief of PKR for the 2022-2026 term in July 2022 and also became the Deputy Youth Chief of the Pakatan Harapan (PH) coalition in the same month. In November 2022, he was elected as the Member of Parliament (MP) for Hang Tuah Jaya. Since December 2022, he has served as the Deputy Minister of Youth and Sports in the Unity Government under Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim. He was a recipient of the 17th Yang di-Pertuan Agong Installation Medal in 2024.

He is a member of the Malaysian Islamic Party (PAS), which is a component party of the Perikatan Nasional (PN) and was formerly part of the Gagasan Sejahtera (GS) and Pakatan Rakyat (PR) coalitions. He has held leadership roles within PAS, serving as the Head of the Dewan Ulamak of PAS since 2021 and State Commissioner of PAS of Kedah since 2019.

biography: Teresa Kok Suh Sim, born 31 March 1964 in Kuala Lumpur, is a Malaysian politician of Chinese Hakka descent and a Catholic. She holds a Bachelor of Communication from Universiti Sains Malaysia (1990) and a Master of Philosophy from University of Malaya. A long-standing member of the Democratic Action Party (DAP), she has served as the Member of Parliament for Seputeh since November 1999 and became Deputy Chairperson of the Public Accounts Committee (PAC) in July 2024. Her past roles include Minister of Primary Industries (July 2018 - February 2020) and Senior Member of the Selangor State Executive Council (EXCO) and MLA for Kinrara (March 2008 - May 2013). She also worked as political secretary to Lim Kit Siang (1990-1995) and writes a weekly column for Sin Chew Daily.

A member of Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR), he has also served as its vice-president (2022-2025), Youth Chief (2014-2018), and Communications Director (2010-2013). He pursued law at King's College London.

name: Hassan Abdul Karim
biography: Hassan bin Abdul Karim is a Malaysian politician and lawyer born on 17 October 1951. He has served as the Member of Parliament for Pasir Gudang since May 2018. A graduate of Universiti Malaya and a lawyer by profession, he began his political career in 1978 with Parti Sosialis Rakyat Malaysia (later Parti Rakyat Malaysia), where he rose to become its youth chief, secretary-general, and later president from April 2005 until his resignation in November 2009. He subsequently re-joined the People's Justice Party (PKR) in November 2009. Within PKR, he served as the Johor state chairman from December 2018 to July 2019. He was also appointed Chairman of the Federal-State Relations Select Committee from December 2018 to November 2022. Hassan successfully defended his Pasir Gudang seat in both the 2018 and 2022 general elections. He was previously charged with sedition in 2015 for statements made on Twitter but was granted a full acquittal in May 2019.

He previously served as the Minister of Domestic Trade and Consumer Affairs from March 2020 to November 2022, and Deputy Minister of Rural and Regional Development from May 2013 to May 2018. He has also served as the Member of Parliament (MP) for Kapit since November 1999. Nanta is the grandson of former Sarawak leaders Tun Jugah anak Barieng and Temenggong Dato' Banyang anak Janting and the son of former politician Tan Sri Leonard Linggi Jugah. He is married to Datin Angelina, daughter of Tan Sri Celestine Ujang Jilan. He is also a businessman, having served as the Chairman of shipping company Swee Joo Bhd. He is the cousin of Wilson Ugak Kumbong.

biography: A native of Ayer Tawar, Perak, Ngeh Koo Ham comes from an old Ayer Tawar Methodist family. His parents were rubber tappers. He was christened James by the late Rev. Koh Gie Bing. As a student, he was active in the Methodist Youth Fellowship and later with the Persatuan Kristian Varsiti (PKV, part of the International Fellowship of Evangelical Students) at Universiti Malaya. He has also served on the Local Church Executive Council (LCEC) of the Ayer Tawar Chinese Methodist Church (ATCMC) and provided legal counsel to the Methodist Church in Malaysia. He is a regular communicant member of ATCMC where he often also provides translation during Sunday worship. He practiced as a lawyer with Nga Hock Cheh and Co. before starting his own law firm, Ngeh and Co, headquartered in Ayer Tawar, with branches in Ipoh, Perak and Kuala Lumpur.

name: Fong Kui Lun
biography: Fong Kui Lun is a Malaysian politician who has served as the Member of Parliament (MP) for Bukit Bintang since November 1999 and Klang from October 1990 to April 1995. He is a member of Democratic Action Party (DAP), a component party of the Pakatan Harapan (PH) and formerly Pakatan Rakyat (PR), Barisan Alternatif (BA) as well as Gagasan Rakyat (GR) coalitions. He has also served as the Treasurer-General of DAP since 1999. He is also presently the oldest MP at the age of 78. Fong was first elected to Parliament in the 1990 general election as the Klang MP. He was not reelected and lost the seat in the 1995 general election to Tan Yee Kew of the Barisan Nasional (BN). He returned to Parliament in 1999 general election, winning the seat of Bukit Bintang, and became the DAP's treasurer in the same year. He was re-elected to Parliament in the consecutive 2004, 2008, 2013, 2018 and 2022 general elections. In 2012, Fong apprehended a snatch thief attempting to escape in Pudu, Kuala Lumpur.

name: Muhyiddin Yassin
biography: Mahiaddin bin Md Yasin, commonly known as Muhyiddin Yassin, is a Malaysian politician who served as the 8th Prime Minister of Malaysia from March 2020 to August 2021. He has been the Member of Parliament for Pagoh since 1978. A long-time member of UMNO, he held several cabinet positions, including Deputy Prime Minister, before being sacked in 2015 for criticising the government's handling of the 1MDB scandal. In 2016, he co-founded Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (BERSATU) and joined the Pakatan Harapan coalition which won the 2018 general election. Following a political crisis in 2020, he was appointed Prime Minister, leading the Perikatan Nasional coalition. His premiership was marked by the COVID-19 pandemic response. He resigned in 2021 after losing his parliamentary majority.

name: Ahmad Johnie Zawawi
biography: Ahmad Johnie bin Zawawi was born in Daro, Mukah, Crown Colony of Sarawak on 1 June 1963. He obtained a bachelor's degree in political science from National University of Malaysia (UKM) in 1986. He worked at the Royal Malaysian Customs Department from 1988 to 2000, and served as the Chief Operating Officer (COO) of The Sarawak Press and Warta Distributions from 2000 to 2004, as well as COO of WHS Holdings from 2004 to 2015. In the 2018 general election, he was nominated by Barisan Nasional (BN) to contest for the Igan federal seat and won, becoming the Member of Parliament (MP) for Igan. On 17 May 2020, he was appointed the Chairman of Indah Water by Prime Minister Muhyiddin Yassin. He defended the Igan seat in the 2022 general election as a Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) candidate and was reelected. He is a member of the Parti Pesaka Bumiputera Bersatu (PBB). He is of Melanau descent and has three children with his wife.

name: Riduan Rubin
biography: Riduan bin Rubin is a Malaysian politician who has served as the Member of Parliament (MP) for Tenom since November 2022. He is a member of the Social Democratic Harmony Party (KDM). He was previously an independent and a member of the Malaysian United Indigenous Party of Sabah (Sabah BERSATU). He is one of two KDM MPs, along with Kota Marudu MP Wetrom Bahanda. He is also the son of Rubin Balang, the Member of the Sabah State Legislative Assembly (MLA) for Kemabong.

name: Pang Hok Liong
biography: Pang Hok Liong, born 22 July 1957 in Labis, Johor, is a Malaysian politician and lawyer. He holds an LLM from University College London and is a Barrister-At-Law of Lincoln's Inn. He has been a practicing lawyer since 1986. He served as the Member of the Johor State Legislative Assembly (MLA) for Bekok from October 1990 to April 1995. After previously contesting and losing in the 2004 and 2008 general elections for the Segamat seat, he was finally elected as the Member of Parliament (MP) for Labis in May 2018, representing the Democratic Action Party (DAP). He created history by winning the traditional Malaysian Chinese Association (MCA)-Barisan Nasional (BN) stronghold. He was involved in a notable incident on 20 June 1991 during the opening of the Johor State Legislative Assembly, where he was assaulted by the Sultan of Johor and the then-Crown Prince.

He served as a Member of the Selangor State Executive Council (EXCO) in the Pakatan Rakyat (PR) and Pakatan Harapan (PH) state administrations under former Menteris Besar Khalid Ibrahim and Azmin Ali from February 2012 to May 2018. He is a member of the Malaysian Islamic Party (PAS), a component party of the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition. He also served as the 15th State Commissioner of PAS of Selangor from 2019 to October 2023.

name: Wan Azizah Wan Ismail
biography: Wan Azizah binti Wan Ismail, born on 3 December 1952 in Singapore, is a prominent Malaysian politician. Educated at the Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland, where she excelled in medicine and became a qualified Ophthalmologist, she served as a government doctor for 14 years. Her political career began following the dismissal and arrest of her husband, Anwar Ibrahim, in 1998, when she spearheaded the Reformasi movement. She founded Parti Keadilan Nasional in 1999, which later merged to form the People's Justice Party (PKR) in 2003, serving as its first president until 2018. Wan Azizah has been a Member of Parliament for Permatang Pauh, Pandan, and currently for Bandar Tun Razak since November 2022. She made history as Malaysia's first female Deputy Prime Minister (2018-2020) and Leader of the Opposition (2008; 2015-2018). She is currently the 1st President of the Pakatan Harapan (PH) coalition since July 2017.

He was a co-founder and the first Youth Chief of the Malaysian United Indigenous Party (BERSATU) from 2016 to 2020. On 9 November 2023, he was found guilty on four charges of corruption related to the funds of BERSATU's youth wing, Armada, and subsequently stepped down as MUDA's president. He announced his departure from MUDA on 10 September 2023 to serve as an independent MP.

He was first elected to Parliament in a 1997 by-election for Teluk Intan, serving until 1999. He later won the Ipoh Barat seat in 2004 and has been the Member of Parliament for this constituency since. He served as the Minister of Human Resources from May 2018 to February 2020 under the Pakatan Harapan administration. He has been a vocal advocate for issues affecting the Malaysian Indian community and has been involved with Parliamentarians for Global Action (PGA), chairing its Malaysian chapter in 2019 and advocating for human rights and international law ratification.

biography: Fadillah bin Yusof was born on 17 April 1962 in Kampung Hilir, Sibu, Sarawak, and is the ninth of 14 siblings. In 1986, he earned a Bachelor of Laws (LLB) in Civil and Sharia law from the University of Malaya. After graduating, he practiced law briefly in Kuala Lumpur before returning to Sarawak, where he provided legal advice to elected representatives and eventually served as an independent legal advisor for Parti Pesaka Bumiputera Bersatu (PBB). He officially joined PBB in 1989.

His victory in the 2022 general election was a significant upset, as he defeated the incumbent MP, Nurul Izzah Anwar. As MP for Permatang Pauh, he has been controversial, attempting to ban the sale of alcohol to non-Muslims at Sunway Carnival Mall and making seditious remarks on social media. Additionally, he sparked outrage in Parliament by claiming that low-income students have 'big appetite' and are resorting to sugar dating. He is a Recipient of the 17th Yang di-Pertuan Agong Installation Medal (2024).

name: Howard Lee Chuan How
biography: Howard Lee Chuan How is a Malaysian politician born in Ipoh in 1983. He received his early education in Malaysia before continuing his secondary and college education in the United Kingdom. His political involvement began at age 16 when he was elected vice president of a student union in the UK. He contested for Norwich City Council under the UK Liberal Democrats in 2010. Lee returned to Malaysia in late 2007 and joined the Democratic Action Party (DAP) in January 2008. He served as the Member of the Perak State Legislative Assembly for Pasir Pinji from May 2013 to November 2022 and was a Perak State Executive Councillor from May 2018 to March 2020. He also served as Youth Chief for DAP and Pakatan Harapan. In 2016, he was elected the 24th President of the International Union of Socialist Youth (IUSY). Since November 2022, he has served as the Member of Parliament for Ipoh Timor. He is currently the State Treasurer of DAP of Perak and will be the National Political Education Director of DAP from March 2025.

He was the chairman of Gerakan Mansuh ISA (GMI), a movement of non-governmental organisations against the Internal Security Act. He has also been associated with IKRAM, an Islamic non-governmental organisation as an activist. He previously served as vice-president of Bersih. In 2019, Syed was appointed state chairman of the Johor branch of the People's Justice Party after the resignation of Hassan Abdul Karim. He was succeeded by Rafizi Ramli in the lead-up to the 2022 Malaysian general election and reappointed in May 2024. He received the 17th Yang di-Pertuan Agong Installation Medal in 2024.

Syahredzan graduated with a LL.B. degree from Cardiff University in 2005 and was admitted as an advocate and solicitor of the High Court of Malaya in October 2007. He began his political career in 2018 as political secretary to Lim Kit Siang.

name: Mohammed Taufiq Johari
biography: Mohammed Taufiq bin Johari (born 6 March 1996) is a Malaysian politician who has served as the Member of Parliament (MP) for Sungai Petani since November 2022. He is a member and the Division Chief of Sungai Petani of the People's Justice Party (PKR), a component party of the Pakatan Harapan (PH) coalition. He also served as one of the Vice Youth Chiefs of PKR since May 2025. He is the son of the Speaker of the Dewan Rakyat, Johari Abdul, and is currently the youngest MP at the age of 29. He is also the sole PH MP in the four states controlled by Perikatan Nasional (Perlis, Kedah, Kelantan and Terengganu). He married Fathiya Ainul Mardhiyah Andri Nopendra on 9 March 2024. He was born in Kuala Lumpur and is the sixth of eight children. He received the 17th Yang di-Pertuan Agong Installation Medal in 2025.
